full_context: In linear elastic fracture mechanics, the stress intensity factor for Mode I (opening mode) crack: \(K_{I} = \sigma\sqrt{\pi a}\). This formula applies to an idealized case of an infinite plate with a central through-crack, and is used to evaluate the stress field intensity at the crack tip and crack propagation tendency.

variable_definitions.name: Mode I stress intensity factor
variable_definitions.meaning: Parameter characterizing the stress field intensity at the crack tip, used to predict crack growth

variable_definitions.name: Far-field stress
variable_definitions.meaning: Uniform stress applied far from the crack region

variable_definitions.name: Half crack length
variable_definitions.meaning: Half the length of a central through-crack

potential_questions: An infinite plate has a central through-crack of length 2a=10 mm, under far-field tensile stress \(\sigma=100\) MPa. Find the stress intensity factor \(K_{I}\).
task_description: 1. Determine the far-field uniform stress \(\sigma\). 2. Determine the half crack length \(a\). 3. Compute \(K_{I} = \sigma\sqrt{\pi a}\). 4. Output \(K_{I}\) with proper unit.

formula: p_{0} = \frac{3F}{2\pi a^{2}},\quad a = \sqrt[3]{\frac{3 F R}{4 E^{*}}}
full_context: Hertz contact theory: expressions for maximum contact pressure \(p_{0}\) and contact radius \(a\) between two elastic bodies (e.g., sphere-on-flat) under normal load. For a sphere on a rigid flat, the equivalent radius \(R=R_{1}\), and equivalent modulus \(E^{*}=E_{1}/(1-\nu_{1}^{2})\).

variable_definitions.name: Maximum Hertz contact pressure
variable_definitions.meaning: Maximum pressure at the center of the contact area between two elastic bodies

variable_definitions.name: Normal load
variable_definitions.meaning: Force perpendicular to the contact surface

variable_definitions.name: Contact radius
variable_definitions.meaning: Radius of the circular contact area

variable_definitions.name: Equivalent radius of curvature
variable_definitions.meaning: \(\frac{1}{R} = \frac{1}{R_{1}} + \frac{1}{R_{2}}\), where \(R_{1}, R_{2}\) are radii of the two contacting bodies

variable_definitions.name: Equivalent elastic modulus
variable_definitions.meaning:
\( rac{1}{E^{*}} =  rac{1-
u_{1}^{2}}{E_{1}} +  rac{1-
u_{2}^{2}}{E_{2}}\), with \(E\) elastic modulus and \(
u\) Poisson’s ratio

potential_questions: A steel ball (\(E_{1}=200\) GPa, \(\nu_{1}=0.3\)) contacts a rigid flat. Ball radius \(R_{1}=10\) mm, normal load \(F=100\) N. Find the maximum contact pressure \(p_{0}\).
task_description: 1. Calculate equivalent radius \(R\) (sphere-on-flat: \(R=R_{1}\)). 2. Calculate equivalent modulus \(E^{*}\) (rigid flat: \(E^{*}=E_{1}/(1-\nu_{1}^{2})\)). 3. Compute contact radius \(a = \sqrt[3]{3FR/(4E^{*})}\). 4. Compute maximum contact pressure \(p_{0} = 3F/(2\pi a^{2})\).

full_context: First critical angular speed of a simplified single-disk rotor model: \(\omega_{\mathrm{cr}} = \sqrt{k/m}\). Resonance occurs when the rotational speed approaches this value, requiring peak avoidance or vibration isolation design.

variable_definitions.name: Critical angular speed
variable_definitions.meaning: Angular speed at which the rotor system resonates, corresponding to its first natural frequency

variable_definitions.name: Shaft stiffness
variable_definitions.meaning: Transverse (bending) stiffness of the shaft at the rotor

variable_definitions.name: Rotor mass
variable_definitions.meaning: Equivalent mass lumped at the rotor

potential_questions: Single-disk rotor mass \(m=50\) kg, shaft transverse stiffness \(k=1.0\times10^{6}\) N/m. Find the critical angular speed \(\omega_{\mathrm{cr}}\).
task_description: 1. Determine transverse equivalent stiffness \(k\). 2. Determine rotor mass \(m\). 3. Compute \(\omega_{\mathrm{cr}} = \sqrt{k/m}\). 4. Output the result.

full_context: Kirchhoff–Love thin plate small-deflection bending equation: \(D\nabla^{4} w = q\), where \(D\) is the bending stiffness of the plate. Valid for thin plates where thickness is much smaller than in-plane dimensions and normals remain perpendicular during deformation.

variable_definitions.name: Plate bending stiffness
variable_definitions.meaning: Resistance of the plate to bending deformation, depending on material and thickness

variable_definitions.name: Transverse deflection
variable_definitions.meaning: Displacement of the plate midplane perpendicular to the plate surface

variable_definitions.name: Transverse distributed load
variable_definitions.meaning: Normal force per unit area acting on the plate

variable_definitions.name: Elastic modulus
variable_definitions.meaning: Material’s resistance to elastic deformation

variable_definitions.name: Plate thickness
variable_definitions.meaning: Thickness of the plate

variable_definitions.name: Poisson’s ratio
variable_definitions.meaning: Ratio of transverse to axial strain

potential_questions: Square steel plate side length 1 m, \(h=5\) mm, \(E=200\) GPa, \(\nu=0.3\), under uniform load \(q=1\) kPa. Find bending stiffness \(D\) and write the governing equation.
potential_questions.task_description: 1. Calculate \(D = \frac{E h^{3}}{12(1-\nu^{2})}\). 2. Write governing equation \(D\nabla^{4} w = q\). 3. Specify boundary conditions for solution.

potential_questions.full_context: In-plane stiffness matrix element \(A_{ij}\) in Classical Lamination Theory (CLT). The equivalent in-plane stiffness of a laminate is obtained by integrating the transformed stiffness \(\bar{Q}^{(k)}\) through the thickness.

variable_definitions.name: In-plane stiffness matrix component
variable_definitions.meaning: Stiffness component relating in-plane forces and in-plane strains of the laminate

variable_definitions.name: Transformed stiffness component of layer k
variable_definitions.meaning: Stiffness component \(Q_{ij}\) of layer \(k\) transformed to the laminate coordinate system

variable_definitions.name: Top coordinate of layer k
variable_definitions.meaning: Distance from the midplane to the top surface of layer \(k\) along thickness

variable_definitions.name: Number of layers
variable_definitions.meaning: Total number of plies in the laminate

potential_questions: Symmetric carbon fiber laminate [0/90/0], each ply thickness \(t=0.125\) mm. Given lamina stiffness \(Q_{11}=150\) GPa, find in-plane stiffness \(A_{11}\).
potential_questions.task_description: 1. Determine layup sequence, ply thickness, and material stiffness \(Q_{ij}\). 2. Perform coordinate transformation to get \(\bar{Q}_{ij}^{(k)}\). 3. Define thickness coordinates \(z_{k}\). 4. Compute \(A_{ij} = \sum \bar{Q}_{ij}^{(k)}(z_{k}-z_{k-1})\).

potential_questions.full_context: von Mises equivalent stress converts a multiaxial stress state into an equivalent uniaxial stress for yield assessment. Used with the von Mises yield criterion.

variable_definitions.name: von Mises equivalent stress
variable_definitions.meaning: Stress value that equates multiaxial stress to uniaxial stress for plastic yield assessment

variable_definitions.name: First principal stress
variable_definitions.meaning: Algebraically largest principal stress at a point

variable_definitions.name: Second principal stress
variable_definitions.meaning: Intermediate principal stress at a point

variable_definitions.name: Third principal stress
variable_definitions.meaning: Algebraically smallest principal stress at a point

potential_questions: Principal stresses \(\sigma_{1}=200\) MPa, \(\sigma_{2}=100\) MPa, \(\sigma_{3}=0\). Find the von Mises equivalent stress \(\sigma_{\mathrm{eq}}\).
potential_questions.task_description: 1. Obtain principal stresses \(\sigma_{1}, \sigma_{2}, \sigma_{3}\). 2. Compute squared differences of each pair. 3. Sum and divide by 2. 4. Take square root to get \(\sigma_{\mathrm{eq}}\).

potential_questions.full_context: Norton–Bailey creep law with Arrhenius temperature term for steady-state creep. Describes the stable deformation rate of a material under constant stress at high temperature.

variable_definitions.name: Steady-state creep strain rate
variable_definitions.meaning: Constant deformation rate under steady stress and high temperature

variable_definitions.name: Material constant
variable_definitions.meaning: Empirical constant related to material microstructure

variable_definitions.name: Applied stress
variable_definitions.meaning: Stress causing creep deformation

variable_definitions.name: Stress exponent
variable_definitions.meaning: Exponent characterizing stress sensitivity of creep

variable_definitions.name: Activation energy
variable_definitions.meaning: Thermal activation energy for creep process

variable_definitions.name: Absolute temperature
variable_definitions.meaning: Temperature in Kelvin

potential_questions.potential_questions: High-temperature alloy: \(A=5.0\times10^{-10}\) (MPa)⁻ⁿ/s, \(n=3\), \(Q=200\) kJ/mol. At \(\sigma=50\) MPa, \(T=1073\) K. Find steady-state creep strain rate \(\dot{\varepsilon}_{c}\).
potential_questions.task_description: 1. Use given \(A, n, Q, \sigma, T\). 2. Compute exponential term \(\exp(-Q/(RT))\). 3. Compute \(\sigma^{n}\). 4. Compute \(\dot{\varepsilon}_{c} = A\sigma^{n}\exp(-Q/(RT))\).

potential_questions.full_context: Archard wear model: wear volume is proportional to normal load and sliding distance, and inversely proportional to the hardness of the softer material. Used for engineering estimation of adhesive wear.

variable_definitions.name: Wear volume
variable_definitions.meaning: Total volume of material lost during sliding

variable_definitions.name: Wear coefficient
variable_definitions.meaning: Dimensionless coefficient characterizing wear tendency of a friction pair

variable_definitions.name: Normal load
variable_definitions.meaning: Force perpendicular to the contact interface

variable_definitions.name: Total sliding distance
variable_definitions.meaning: Total relative sliding length of the friction pair

variable_definitions.name: Hardness of the softer material
variable_definitions.meaning: Resistance to local plastic deformation (indentation)

potential_questions: Steel-on-steel sliding: \(K=1\times10^{-5}\), \(F_{n}=100\) N, \(s=1000\) m, \(H=2\) GPa. Find wear volume \(V\).
potential_questions.task_description: 1. Determine \(K, F_{n}, s, H\). 2. Compute \(V = K F_{n} s / H\). 3. Output result with unit conversion.

potential_questions.formula: \frac{\partial}{\partial x}\!\left(h^{3}\,\frac{\partial p}{\partial x}\right) + \frac{\partial}{\partial z}\!\left(h^{3}\,\frac{\partial p}{\partial z}\right) = 6\mu U\,\frac{\partial h}{\partial x}
potential_questions.full_context: Two-dimensional Reynolds equation for hydrodynamic lubrication: pressure distribution in a thin oil film is driven by film thickness gradient and surface velocity. Reduces to 1D for infinitely long bearings.

variable_definitions.name: Film pressure
variable_definitions.meaning: Hydrodynamic pressure in the lubricating film

variable_definitions.name: Film thickness
variable_definitions.meaning: Local thickness of the lubricant film normal to the surface

variable_definitions.name: Dynamic viscosity
variable_definitions.meaning: Fluid resistance to shear deformation

variable_definitions.name: Sliding velocity
variable_definitions.meaning: Relative velocity of the moving surfaces

variable_definitions.name: Coordinate directions
variable_definitions.meaning: Coordinates along sliding and transverse directions

potential_questions: 1D infinitely long bearing: \(\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{d}x}\!\left(h^{3}\,\frac{\mathrm{d}p}{\mathrm{d}x}\right) = 6\mu U\,\frac{\mathrm{d}h}{\mathrm{d}x}\). Given \(h(x)\), derive pressure distribution \(p(x)\).
potential_questions.task_description: 1. Use given \(h(x), \mu, U\). 2. Integrate once and apply \(p=0\) boundary condition. 3. Integrate again to get \(p(x)\). 4. Check non-negative pressure and boundary consistency.

potential_questions.full_context: Thermal stress under fully constrained linear elasticity: stress arises when thermal expansion is prevented, approximated by \(\sigma_{\mathrm{th}} = E \alpha \Delta T\).

variable_definitions.name: Thermal stress
variable_definitions.meaning: Stress induced by temperature change under constrained deformation

variable_definitions.name: Elastic modulus
variable_definitions.meaning: Material’s resistance to elastic deformation

variable_definitions.name: Linear thermal expansion coefficient
variable_definitions.meaning: Relative length change per 1 K temperature change

variable_definitions.name: Temperature change
variable_definitions.meaning: Temperature difference relative to reference state

potential_questions: Steel rod fixed at both ends: \(E=200\) GPa, \(\alpha=12\times10^{-6}\) /K, \(\Delta T=100\) K. Find thermal stress \(\sigma_{\mathrm{th}}\).
potential_questions.task_description: 1. Determine \(E, \alpha, \Delta T\). 2. Compute \(\sigma_{\mathrm{th}} = E \alpha \Delta T\). 3. Note sign and constraint effect on actual stress.

potential_questions.full_context: Definition of sound pressure level: \(L_{p}\) expresses sound intensity in decibels relative to the reference sound pressure \(p_{0}=20\,\mu\mathrm{Pa}\) in air.

variable_definitions.name: Sound pressure level
variable_definitions.meaning: 20 times base-10 logarithm of the ratio of measured sound pressure to reference pressure

variable_definitions.name: Measured sound pressure
variable_definitions.meaning: Effective sound pressure at a point in the sound field

variable_definitions.name: Reference sound pressure
variable_definitions.meaning: Typically \(20\,\mu\mathrm{Pa}\) in air (approximate threshold of hearing)

potential_questions: Measured sound pressure \(p=0.1\) Pa in air, reference \(p_{0}=20\,\mu\mathrm{Pa}\). Find \(L_{p}\).
potential_questions.task_description: 1. Obtain \(p\) and \(p_{0}\). 2. Compute ratio \(p/p_{0}\). 3. Compute \(\log_{10}(p/p_{0})\). 4. Multiply by 20 to get \(L_{p}\).

potential_questions.full_context: Time-domain control law of PID controller, composed of proportional, integral, and derivative terms. Used to eliminate steady-state error and improve dynamic performance.

variable_definitions.name: Control output
variable_definitions.meaning: Control signal generated by the controller

variable_definitions.name: Error signal
variable_definitions.meaning: Difference between setpoint and measured value

variable_definitions.name: Proportional gain
variable_definitions.meaning: Linear coefficient between control output and error

variable_definitions.name: Integral gain
variable_definitions.meaning: Coefficient between control output and integral of error

variable_definitions.name: Derivative gain
variable_definitions.meaning: Coefficient between control output and derivative of error

potential_questions: PID gains \(K_{p}=2\), \(K_{i}=0.5\), \(K_{d}=0.1\), error \(e(t)=1-\exp(-t)\). Find control output \(u(2)\) at \(t=2\).
potential_questions.task_description: 1. Determine error \(e(t)\). 2. Compute proportional term \(K_{p}e(t)\). 3. Compute integral term \(K_{i}\int_{0}^{t} e(\tau)d\tau\). 4. Compute derivative term \(K_{d}\frac{de}{dt}\). 5. Sum three terms for \(u(t)\). 6. Evaluate at \(t=2\).

potential_questions.full_context: Cumulative failure probability of the Weibull distribution. Used to compute the probability of failure by time \(t\) given shape parameter \(\beta\) and scale parameter \(\eta\).

variable_definitions.name: Cumulative failure probability
variable_definitions.meaning: Probability that the item fails before time \(t\)

variable_definitions.name: Time
variable_definitions.meaning: Operating or service time

variable_definitions.name: Characteristic life
variable_definitions.meaning: Scale parameter; equals mean life for exponential distribution (\(\beta=1\))

variable_definitions.name: Shape parameter
variable_definitions.meaning: Parameter defining the shape of the failure rate curve

potential_questions: Mechanical part follows Weibull distribution: \(\eta=1000\) h, \(\beta=2\). Find \(F(500)\) at \(t=500\) h.

potential_questions.full_context: Sensitivity analysis: partial derivative of objective function \(f(\mathbf{x})\) with respect to design variable \(x_{i}\) evaluates its influence on the objective and provides gradient direction for optimization.

variable_definitions.name: Objective function
variable_definitions.meaning: Function to be minimized or maximized in optimization

variable_definitions.name: i-th design variable
variable_definitions.meaning: Adjustable independent parameter in the optimization problem

variable_definitions.name: Sensitivity of f w.r.t. x_i
variable_definitions.meaning: Rate of change of objective with respect to design variable \(x_{i}\)

potential_questions: Objective function \(f(x_{1},x_{2}) = x_{1}^{2} + 3x_{1}x_{2} + 2x_{2}^{2}\). Find \(\partial f/\partial x_{1}\).
potential_questions.task_description: 1. Given \(f(\mathbf{x})\). 2. Take partial derivative with respect to \(x_{i}\). 3. Output \(\partial f/\partial x_{i}\), optionally with numerical evaluation.

potential_questions.full_context: Volume integral expression for element stiffness matrix. Formed by integrating the bilinear form of strain-displacement matrix \([B]\) and constitutive matrix \([D]\) over the element domain.

variable_definitions.name: Element stiffness matrix
variable_definitions.meaning: Matrix relating nodal forces to nodal displacements in a finite element

variable_definitions.name: Strain-displacement matrix
variable_definitions.meaning: Geometric matrix relating nodal displacements to element strains

variable_definitions.name: Constitutive matrix
variable_definitions.meaning: Matrix describing material stress–strain relation

variable_definitions.name: Element volume
variable_definitions.meaning: Volume occupied by the finite element

potential_questions: 2D plane-stress constant-strain triangle. Given \([B]\) and \([D]\), write and explain the element stiffness matrix integral \([K]^{e}\).
potential_questions.task_description: 1. Determine shape functions from element type. 2. Derive \([B]\). 3. Provide \([D]\) from material model. 4. Integrate \([B]^{T}[D][B]\) over \(V^{e}\) to get \([K]^{e}\).

potential_questions.full_context: Matrix differential equation of motion for multi-degree-of-freedom linear systems, where \([M]\), \([C]\), \([K]\) are mass, damping, stiffness matrices; \(\{x\}\) displacement vector; \(\{F(t)\}\) external force vector.

variable_definitions.name: Mass matrix
variable_definitions.meaning: Matrix relating inertial forces to accelerations in multi-DOF system

variable_definitions.name: Damping matrix
variable_definitions.meaning: Matrix relating damping forces to velocities

variable_definitions.name: Stiffness matrix
variable_definitions.meaning: Matrix relating elastic forces to displacements

variable_definitions.name: Displacement vector
variable_definitions.meaning: Column vector of displacements for all DOFs

variable_definitions.name: External force vector
variable_definitions.meaning: Column vector of applied forces on each DOF

potential_questions: Two-DOF spring–mass–damper system. Given \([M], [K], [C], \{F(t)\}\), write the matrix equation of motion.
potential_questions.task_description: 1. Provide \([M], [K], [C]\). 2. Provide \(\{F(t)\}\). 3. Write \([M]\{\ddot{x}\} + [C]\{\dot{x}\} + [K]\{x\} = \{F(t)\}\). 4. Specify initial conditions for solution.

potential_questions.full_context: Pump head is defined as total mechanical energy gain per unit weight of fluid, composed of pressure, velocity, and elevation terms.

variable_definitions.name: Pump head
variable_definitions.meaning: Work done by pump per unit weight of fluid, expressed as total head increase

variable_definitions.name: Inlet and outlet pressure
variable_definitions.meaning: Static pressure at pump inlet and outlet

variable_definitions.name: Inlet and outlet velocity
variable_definitions.meaning: Average flow velocity at inlet and outlet pipes

variable_definitions.name: Inlet and outlet elevation
variable_definitions.meaning: Height of inlet/outlet center relative to datum

variable_definitions.name: Fluid density
variable_definitions.meaning: Mass per unit volume of fluid

variable_definitions.name: Gravitational acceleration
variable_definitions.meaning: Approximately \(9.81\) m/s²

potential_questions.task_description: 1. Use \(p_{1},p_{2},v_{1},v_{2},z_{1},z_{2},\rho,g\). 2. Compute three components. 3. Sum to get \(H\). 4. Maintain consistent units.

potential_questions.full_context: Newton’s law of cooling: convective heat flux between a solid surface and fluid flow is proportional to temperature difference, with proportionality constant \(h\) (convective heat transfer coefficient).

variable_definitions.name: Convective heat flux
variable_definitions.meaning: Heat transfer rate per unit surface area

variable_definitions.name: Convective heat transfer coefficient
variable_definitions.meaning: Coefficient characterizing convective intensity, dependent on flow and geometry

variable_definitions.name: Surface temperature
variable_definitions.meaning: Temperature of solid wall in contact with fluid

variable_definitions.name: Fluid bulk temperature
variable_definitions.meaning: Temperature of fluid far from the solid surface

potential_questions: Flat plate surface \(T_{s}=80\) °C, air flow \(T_{\infty}=20\) °C, \(h=15\) W/(m²·K). Find heat flux \(q\).
potential_questions.task_description: 1. Determine \(h, T_{s}, T_{\infty}\). 2. Compute \(\Delta T = T_{s} - T_{\infty}\). 3. Compute \(q = h\Delta T\). 4. Output \(q\).

potential_questions.full_context: Error propagation formula for independent variables (linear approximation): variance of function \(f\) is approximated by sum of weighted variances of inputs, used for uncertainty analysis and precision design.

variable_definitions.name: Standard deviation of f
variable_definitions.meaning: Total uncertainty of \(f\) due to input variations

variable_definitions.name: Objective function
variable_definitions.meaning: Quantity determined by independent variables \(x_{i}\)

variable_definitions.name: i-th independent variable
variable_definitions.meaning: Input parameter affecting \(f\)

variable_definitions.name: Standard deviation of x_i
variable_definitions.meaning: Uncertainty or error of variable \(x_{i}\)

potential_questions.task_description: 1. Define \(f(x_{1},...,x_{n})\). 2. Compute partial derivatives \(\partial f/\partial x_{i}\). 3. Substitute \(\sigma_{x_{i}}\) to compute each term. 4. Sum to get \(\sigma_{f}^{2}\). 5. Take square root for \(\sigma_{f}\).

potential_questions.full_context: Approximate electrostatic attractive force between parallel-plate capacitors, used in MEMS electrostatic actuator design. Neglects fringing fields and non-uniformity.

variable_definitions.name: Parallel-plate electrostatic force
variable_definitions.meaning: Attractive force between charged parallel plates due to voltage difference

variable_definitions.name: Vacuum permittivity
variable_definitions.meaning: Approximately \(8.854\times10^{-12}\) F/m

variable_definitions.name: Relative permittivity
variable_definitions.meaning: Dielectric constant relative to vacuum

variable_definitions.name: Overlap area
variable_definitions.meaning: Effective facing area between two parallel plates

variable_definitions.name: Applied voltage
variable_definitions.meaning: Potential difference between plates

variable_definitions.name: Plate gap
variable_definitions.meaning: Distance between two parallel plates

potential_questions: MEMS parallel-plate actuator: \(A=100\,\mu\mathrm{m} \times 100\,\mu\mathrm{m}\), \(d=1\,\mu\mathrm{m}\), \(\varepsilon_{r}\approx1\), \(V=10\) V. Find electrostatic force \(F\).
potential_questions.task_description: 1. Calculate area \(A\). 2. Use given \(d, \varepsilon_{r}, V\). 3. Compute \(F = \frac12 \varepsilon_0 \varepsilon_r A V^2 / d^2\). 4. Convert units properly (μm → m).
